vision_profile.current_state: Aarav is a Backend Engineer with 6 years of experience, proficient in Java, Spring Boot, REST APIs, PostgreSQL, and Redis.
vision_profile.main_blocker: His biggest fear is that weak system design depth will prevent him from advancing to senior engineering roles and owning larger architectural decisions.
vision_profile.top_motivation: He is highly motivated to move into a senior backend role to take ownership of larger engineering decisions and contribute to architectural strategy.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: You will establish a strong understanding of core system design concepts, networking fundamentals, and database principles essential for building robust backend systems.
milestones.identity_statement: You are laying the groundwork to become a confident system architect, building upon your existing backend expertise.

science.desc: Design a URL shortener: identify core components, discuss trade-offs for different consistency models, and propose initial scalability strategies.

modules.title: Databases & Storage
lessons: NoSQL Database Types (Key-Value, Document, Columnar, Graph)

science.desc: Explain the trade-offs between SQL and NoSQL databases. When would you choose one over the other for a new service?

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: You will grasp the core challenges and solutions in distributed systems, including fault tolerance, data consistency, and basic scalability patterns.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming adept at designing systems that can withstand failures and scale to meet growing demands.

scenario.title: Designing for Atomicity in Distributed Systems

science.desc: A distributed system experiences network partition. Describe how different consistency models (e.g., eventual, strong) would behave and how you'd design for fault tolerance.

milestones.label: M03
milestones.o: You will master advanced patterns for building resilient and observable distributed systems, including event-driven architectures and microservices.
milestones.identity_statement: You are evolving into an architect who designs robust, self-healing, and highly available systems.

scenario.title: Setting up Alerts for a Critical Service

science.desc: Describe how you would implement a robust logging and tracing solution for a microservices architecture to quickly diagnose issues.
You need to migrate a monolithic e-commerce application to microservices. Propose a decomposition strategy and discuss how you'd handle inter-service communication and data consistency.

milestones.label: M04
milestones.o: You will gain practical experience with AWS deployment patterns, cloud services, and security best practices to build scalable and secure cloud-native applications.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a cloud-savvy engineer, leveraging the power of modern infrastructure to deploy and manage complex systems.

modules.title: Cloud & Deployment (AWS Focus)
lessons: Introduction to Cloud Computing (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS)
AWS Core Services (EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda)

scenario.title: Setting up a Basic AWS Environment

science.desc: Design a highly available and scalable web application on AWS using EC2, RDS, and S3. Detail the deployment strategy and how you'd ensure fault tolerance.

scenario.title: Securing a REST API with OAuth

milestones.label: M05
milestones.o: You will translate high-level system designs into robust, maintainable, and performant code using OOP principles, design patterns, and effective API design.
milestones.identity_statement: You are refining your craft, ensuring that every line of code contributes to a resilient and well-architected system.

scenario.title: Applying SOLID to a Design Problem

science.desc: Explain the SOLID principles with real-world code examples. How do they contribute to maintainable and scalable software?
Design a public API for a new payment service. Consider authentication, error handling, idempotency, and versioning. Justify your choices.

milestones.label: M06
milestones.t: System Design Case Studies & Interview Prep
milestones.o: You will apply your comprehensive system design knowledge to solve complex, real-world problems through case studies and refine your interview skills.
milestones.identity_statement: You are confidently tackling complex architectural challenges, ready to articulate your design decisions in any senior engineering setting.

science.desc: Design a global real-time analytics dashboard. Discuss data ingestion, processing, storage, and visualization components, focusing on latency and consistency.
Explain common concurrency issues like deadlocks and race conditions. How do you prevent and detect them in a multi-threaded application?

milestones.label: M07
milestones.o: You will develop the skills to lead engineering initiatives, mentor junior engineers, and create comprehensive technical design documentation, solidifying your role as a senior architect.
milestones.identity_statement: You are stepping into a leadership role, guiding teams and shaping the technical direction of projects with clarity and vision.

scenario.title: Writing a Design Document for a New Feature
Mentoring a Junior Engineer on a Complex Task

science.desc: You're tasked with writing a technical design document for a new critical service. Outline the key sections you would include and why each is important.
